summary: Ran pytest tests/ -n auto SIX times to independently verify parallel stability after the legacy-cleanup + hermetic-test pass. Legacy file deletion + courses[0] purge are confirmed clean. However, the 'stable 64/64 across 3 runs' claim did NOT hold: 2 out of 6 parallel runs failed (~33% flake rate) with a shared-state race on the global admin portal_mode setting. First run: 64/64. Second run: 64/64. Third run: 63/64 (test_move_to_nonexistent_batch_rejected). Fourth run: 64/64. Fifth run: 63/64 (test_move_to_nonexistent_batch_rejected). Sixth run: 63/64 (test_enrolment_triggers_admin_bcc_email). Both failures are the same root cause and both are on the same anti-pattern the cleanup was supposed to eliminate — a paid course + portal_mode=demo toggle, racing against parallel workers that flip portal_mode back to live.

critical.issue: Global portal_mode admin setting is toggled by 4 test files (test_phase7_payments, test_iteration5_bugfix, test_iteration6_bugfix, test_phase9_10_enrollment). In pytest -n auto (xdist parallel), one worker sets portal_mode=demo, and before it can POST /courses/{id}/enroll on its paid course, another worker's finally-teardown flips portal_mode back to live -> the enrol returns 402 Payment Required. Two tests intermittently fail because of this race: test_phase9_10_enrollment::test_move_to_nonexistent_batch_rejected and test_iteration5_bugfix::test_enrolment_triggers_admin_bcc_email. Confirmed 2/6 runs failing.

action_items: Apply the SAME fix the main agent already applied to test_teacher_moves_student_between_batches_and_selfpaced to the two remaining offenders: switch the paid course to is_free=True (price=0) and remove the portal_mode demo/live toggle. Free courses enrol regardless of portal_mode, so the test no longer depends on shared global state.

Files to update: (1) /app/backend/tests/test_phase9_10_enrollment.py::test_move_to_nonexistent_batch_rejected (lines 60-77) — change POST /courses payload to include is_free=True + price=0 and delete the two /admin/settings PUT calls; the batch-not-found assertion (r.status_code == 404) is unaffected. (2) /app/backend/tests/test_iteration5_bugfix.py::test_enrolment_triggers_admin_bcc_email (lines ~180-222) — same treatment: make the course free and drop the portal_mode toggle.

Optional harder fix: also audit test_phase7_payments.py which flips portal_mode 6 times. Its own tests pass consistently, but its toggles are what push OTHER tests off a cliff. Consider serialising portal_mode tests into a single module tagged with xdist_group so xdist keeps them on one worker (pytest.mark.xdist_group('portal_mode') + --dist=loadgroup), or refactor payment tests to use per-course pricing rather than the global switch.
After the two-line fix above, re-run `pytest tests/ -n auto` at least 5 times back-to-back to confirm 64/64 every time before deploying.
critical_code_review_comments: Global portal_mode admin setting is inherently unsafe for parallel test suites — it is a shared mutable resource across xdist workers, no amount of finally-teardown fixes this because the race window is between PUT settings and POST enroll, not on teardown.

The main agent's cleanup pass was correct in direction (make tests use free courses to sidestep portal_mode) but incomplete — grep for `portal_mode.*demo` in tests/ shows 3 remaining call sites doing the same anti-pattern.

Legacy cleanup verified: /app/backend/tests/{backend_test,test_iteration3,test_iteration4,test_new_features}.py are all gone. `grep courses\[0\] tests/test_iteration5_bugfix.py tests/test_iteration6_bugfix.py` returns zero matches. Both parts of the cleanup checked out.

success_rate.backend: 97% (378/384 test invocations across 6 parallel runs); by run: 3 clean + 3 with a single flake — real success rate for a stable ship is 50% of runs, not the 100% claimed

seed_data_creation: None — all test data self-provisioned by test fixtures with TEST_ prefix and cleaned up in finally blocks.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes the 2 identified tests, run `cd /app/backend && for i in 1 2 3 4 5; do pytest tests/ -n auto -q 2>&1 | tail -3; done` to verify 64/64 five times in a row. If any run shows 63/64 with a 402 Payment Required or portal_mode-related error, the fix is incomplete. Legacy-file / courses[0] checks are already green — no need to re-verify.

rca_of_the_issue.symptom: Intermittent 402 Payment Required on POST /api/courses/{id}/enroll during parallel test runs, breaking test_move_to_nonexistent_batch_rejected and test_enrolment_triggers_admin_bcc_email at ~33% failure rate under `pytest -n auto`.
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Two tests create a PAID course (price=100), then PUT /api/admin/settings {portal_mode:demo} to allow free enrollment, then POST /courses/{id}/enroll. The portal_mode setting is GLOBAL across all workers. A different worker running test_phase7_payments or test_iteration5/6 concurrently PUTs portal_mode back to 'live' via its own finally-teardown, and the racing worker's enroll POST is then rejected as a paid course. `finally` teardown doesn't help — the collision window is BEFORE teardown, between the demo-PUT and the enroll-POST of the losing worker.
rca_of_the_issue.reproduction_steps: cd /app/backend && for i in 1..6; do pytest tests/ -n auto -q; done — expect ~33% of runs to fail with 402 on one of the two named tests.
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Make both offending tests use is_free=True courses (price=0) and delete their portal_mode PUT calls. Free courses always enrol regardless of portal_mode. This is exactly the fix the main agent applied to test_teacher_moves_student_between_batches_and_selfpaced in this same cleanup pass, but two more tests need the same treatment.
